mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques for memory management and request batching. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM easily.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This adds the vLLM repository for version 0.18.0.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function correctly within your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource for vLLM to serve a model, allowing you to test the installation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This creates a simple vLLM resource to serve the 'gpt-3' model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> This command lists the pods in the 'vllm' namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and its associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss if persistent volumes are used.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and data. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Drain the node to safely upgrade.
> This command upgrades vLLM to version 0.18.0.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the repository is not added.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' to refresh the repo and ensure vLLM is installed.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM application may be misconfigured or the model is not available.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for vLLM.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests in the vLLM CR.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service type is set to 'LoadBalancer' or 'NodePort' in the vLLM configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM pods running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n vllm'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that you have the necessary tools installed.
